content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

All four amendments identify real defects in the bead materialization path. fr-6-cff9be0a exposes a critical end-to-end break: the proposal-ref to br-id mapping is in-memory only, so br's repo-prefixed IDs never match the plan.json refs that the controller/CLI check against. fr-6-cf4eb196 and fr-6-8aba633b flag the same duplicate-workstream identity bug with increasing specificity — workstream epics lack the per-proposal disambiguator that task beads get, so valid bundles (permitted by validate()) with duplicate workstream names succeed on first export and hard-fail on re-export. fr-6-684da846 correctly observes that the bead export attempt reservation leaks its state file on error paths, creating avoidable operator friction even though liveness checks eventually recover it.

## Votes

- `fr-6-cff9be0a`: Accept
  Verified against service.rs:1569-1771 and run.rs:294-333/452-464/721-744 and milestone.rs:1530-1591: proposal_id_map is built in materialize_beads and discarded; the returned BeadMaterializationReport exposes only flat ID lists. Downstream membership checks compare br-generated IDs (format <repo>-<slug>, e.g. `ralph-burning-0d1`) against plan.json refs (format `<milestone>.bead-N` or explicit short IDs). canonicalize_milestone_bead_ref would rewrite a br id like `ralph-burning-0d1` to `<milestone>.ralph-burning-0d1`, which still will not equal any plan ref. That makes milestone next/run find zero matching ready beads after a successful export — a functional showstopper that the remediation (persist a durable proposal-ref -> live-bead-id map or enforce stable plan IDs) directly fixes.
- `fr-6-cf4eb196`: Accept
  Verified: workstream epics in service.rs:1598-1614 pass proposal_label: None, while task beads get proposal_export_label(&canonical_proposal_id). bundle.validate() does not require unique workstream names (the test plan_md_disambiguates_duplicate_workstream_names at bundle.rs:3273-3302 confirms duplicates are intentionally allowed and rendered with ordinal suffixes). On a first run with two same-title workstreams, both match nothing in existing_by_title and two distinct beads are created; on re-run, existing_by_title groups them both under the same normalized title, find_existing_materialized_bead sees active_eligible_indices.len() > 1 at service.rs:2023-2036 and returns MilestoneOperationFailed. A plan that validates and exports once therefore stops being re-exportable, which directly contradicts the documented idempotency contract.
- `fr-6-684da846`: Accept
  Verified in cli/milestone.rs:382-423: clear_bead_export_attempt is called only after materialize_beads and record_beads_exported_event both succeed. Any error propagated via `?` leaves the attempt file (with the current PID) on disk. The existing liveness check does eventually mark the attempt stale after the process exits, but the intermediate period — and the user-visible error message 'bead export is already running in another process (pid N)' when PIDs get reused quickly on busy hosts — is avoidable. An RAII guard whose Drop clears the attempt unless an explicit commit() was called keeps the documented idempotency contract symmetric across success and failure paths without changing the happy-path behavior.
- `fr-6-8aba633b`: Accept
  Same root cause as fr-6-cf4eb196, with a concrete, well-scoped remediation. Workstream epics need the same kind of stable, per-occurrence identity that task beads already get via canonical_export_proposal_id + proposal_export_label. Attaching a `workstream:<canonical-id>` label (e.g. derived from title + positional index, matching render_workstream_labels' ordinal scheme at bundle.rs:1283-1311) and consulting it in find_existing_materialized_bead fixes both failure modes named in the amendment: (a) two distinct beads collapsing under the same title key on re-run, and (b) silent collapse of two same-title workstreams into one parent on the first run if reuse ordering ever changes. Keeping proposal_id_map keyed by normalized title for intra-plan dependency resolution is correct because depends_on references use titles, not the new label — so the remediation is low-risk and targeted.
